question_text: A spring stretches 3 cm when a 6 N force is applied. A second, identical spring is added in series with the first. If a 6 N force is now applied to the combination, what is the total extension of both springs together?
explanation: Each spring has spring constant k = 6 N ÷ 0.03 m = 200 N/m. When springs are in series, they both experience the same 6 N force. Each extends by 3 cm under this force, so the total extension is 3 cm + 3 cm = 6 cm.
